title: Add expert-session:auto-switch IPC channel and preload bridge
spec:
Add the IPC channel constant for expert session auto-switch and expose it through the preload bridge so the renderer can receive the event.

## Files to change
- `packages/desktop/src/main/ipc-channels.ts` — add `export const EXPERT_SESSION_AUTO_SWITCH = 'expert-session:auto-switch';`
- `packages/desktop/src/main/preload.ts` — expose a listener (e.g. `onExpertSessionAutoSwitch`) on the zazig bridge so App.tsx can subscribe to this event

## What NOT to do
- Do not change poller.ts, App.tsx, or PipelineColumn.tsx in this job
- Do not wire up any logic — just the channel constant and preload exposure
acceptance_tests:
Given the IPC channels file
When I look for expert-session:auto-switch
Then an exported constant EXPERT_SESSION_AUTO_SWITCH with value 'expert-session:auto-switch' exists

Given the preload bridge
When the renderer calls window.zazig.onExpertSessionAutoSwitch(callback)
Then the callback is registered to receive expert-session:auto-switch events from the main process

title: Route expert auto-switch through IPC in poller.ts and reset on company switch
spec:
Two changes to the main process:

1. In `packages/desktop/src/main/poller.ts` — in `syncExpertSessions`, replace the direct `pty.attach(session)` call with broadcasting the `EXPERT_SESSION_AUTO_SWITCH` IPC event (e.g. `win.webContents.send(EXPERT_SESSION_AUTO_SWITCH, sessionId)`) so the renderer handles the attach via the transition queue. Import `EXPERT_SESSION_AUTO_SWITCH` from ipc-channels.ts.

2. In `packages/desktop/src/main/index.ts` (or wherever `SELECT_COMPANY` IPC is handled) — call `resetExpertSessionTracking()` when the `SELECT_COMPANY` event is received, in addition to on poller stop. This prevents stale session IDs from a previous company triggering false auto-switches.

## Constraint
- Must NOT call pty.attach() directly from poller.ts anymore — the renderer owns the attach lifecycle
acceptance_tests:
Given syncExpertSessions in poller.ts
When a new expert session is detected
Then win.webContents.send is called with EXPERT_SESSION_AUTO_SWITCH and the session ID
And pty.attach() is NOT called directly

Given the SELECT_COMPANY IPC handler in main
When a company switch event is received
Then resetExpertSessionTracking() is called
And subsequent expert session detection starts fresh without stale IDs

title: Handle expert auto-switch IPC in App.tsx and add onExpertClick prop
spec:
In `packages/desktop/src/renderer/App.tsx`:

1. **IPC listener** — Subscribe to `window.zazig.onExpertSessionAutoSwitch` (added in Job 1). When the event fires, enqueue a transition via `transitionQueueRef.current = transitionQueueRef.current.then(async () => { ... })` that detaches the previous session and attaches the new expert session, then calls `setActiveSession(sessionId)` and sets `activeSessionRef.current = sessionId`.

2. **onExpertClick handler** — Create a handler (e.g. `handleExpertClick`) that routes through the same `transitionQueueRef` promise chain, calling `terminalAttach(sessionId)` and updating `activeSession`/`activeSessionRef.current`. Pass this as `onExpertClick` prop to `<PipelineColumn>`.

## Constraints
- Must mirror the existing job card transition pattern exactly
- Must not break existing job switching (activeSessionRef must be correctly set before any new transition starts)
- Clean up the IPC listener on component unmount
acceptance_tests:
Given App.tsx is mounted
When an expert-session:auto-switch IPC event fires with a session ID
Then the transition is enqueued via transitionQueueRef
And activeSession state is updated to the expert session ID
And activeSessionRef.current equals the expert session ID after the transition

Given App.tsx provides onExpertClick to PipelineColumn
When onExpertClick is called with a session ID
Then the transition is enqueued via transitionQueueRef
And activeSession and activeSessionRef.current are updated

Given an active expert session
When a job card is subsequently clicked
Then the expert session is correctly detached (activeSessionRef had the right value)

title: Replace inline terminalAttach and add active highlight for expert cards in PipelineColumn.tsx
spec:
In `packages/desktop/src/renderer/components/PipelineColumn.tsx`:

1. **onExpertClick prop** — Add `onExpertClick?: (sessionId: string) => void` to the component's props interface. Replace the two inline `bridge?.terminalAttach(session.sessionId)` calls in the expert session card's onClick/onKeyDown handlers with `onExpertClick?.(session.sessionId)`.

2. **Active highlight** — Add an `isActive` check for expert session cards: `const isActive = Boolean(activeSession && session.sessionId && activeSession === session.sessionId)`. Apply the same blue highlight class/style as job cards when `isActive` is true.

## Constraint
- Do not remove the `bridge` prop or break anything related to job cards
- The `terminalAttach` bridge prop may still be needed for other uses — only remove the expert session inline calls
acceptance_tests:
Given PipelineColumn receives onExpertClick and activeSession props
When an expert session card is clicked
Then onExpertClick is called with that session's ID
And bridge.terminalAttach is NOT called directly for expert sessions

Given activeSession equals an expert session's sessionId
When PipelineColumn renders
Then that expert session card shows the blue active highlight
And no other expert session card is highlighted

Given activeSession is a job's sessionName
When PipelineColumn renders
Then expert session cards show no highlight and job card shows highlight
And existing job card switching behavior is unchanged
